mission.title: harbor: Proxy cache serves the stale digests for the latest tags
mission.description: Proxy cache serves the stale digests for the latest tags. This issue affects 16+ users.

steps.title: Identify harbor troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your harbor deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review harbor configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant harbor configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor
kubectl get configmap -n harbor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=harbor
```
When a new image is pushed on the **same** tag (e.g., **latest**), the harbor proxy cache doesn't update to the latest digest and still serves the stale one.

steps.title: Enable cache expiration and upgrade Harbor
steps.description:
Configure cache expiration to prevent stale digests and upgrade Harbor:
```bash
# Check current cache configuration in harbor.yml
kubectl get configmap -n harbor harbor-core -o yaml | grep -A 10 'cache'
```sql
Update harbor.yml cache settings:
```yaml
cache:
  enabled: true
  expire_hours: 24
```
Then upgrade Harbor to a version that includes the proxy cache fix:
```bash
helm repo update harbor
helm upgrade harbor harbor/harbor -n harbor -f values.yaml
kubectl rollout status deployment/harbor-core -n harbor
```

steps.title: Confirm Proxy cache serves the stale digests for the… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n harbor --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n harbor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: The proxy cache serves stale digests for mutable tags like 'latest' because Harbor caches manifest list responses and does not re-validate them against the upstream registry when the tag is updated. The fix adds cache invalidation logic that checks the upstream registry for updated digests on mutable tags, ensuring the proxy cache serves the latest manifest when a tag is overwritten upstream.
codeSnippets:
# Upgrade Harbor to include proxy cache fix
helm repo update harbor
helm upgrade harbor harbor/harbor -n harbor -f values.yaml
kubectl rollout status deployment/harbor-core -n harbor

# Verify cached image digest matches upstream
docker pull <harbor-proxy>/<project>/<image>:latest
docker inspect <harbor-proxy>/<project>/<image>:latest | jq '.[0].RepoDigests'

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
